rows.description: The top-level rows of the layout. Must contain at least one row.

parentLayoutLegacyMode.description: When true, uses legacy layout mode inherited from the parent. Set to false for components created with AP 4.2 or later.

LayoutEditorItemType.description: The type of a layout item: 'row' for a row container, 'view' for a node view or widget, 'nestedLayout' for an embedded sub-component layout, 'html' for raw HTML content.

LayoutEditorViewResizeMethod.description: Controls how the view iframe is resized. Aspect-ratio methods maintain proportions; view* methods use the iframe-resizer library to fit content; 'manual' uses fixed minHeight/maxHeight.

nodeID.description: ID of the node inside the component whose view or widget to render.

useLegacyMode.description: When true, renders with legacy iframe styling instead of shadow DOM.

resizeMethod.description: Controls how the view iframe is resized. See LayoutEditorViewResizeMethod for options.

autoResize.description: When true, the iframe automatically resizes to fit its content.

scrolling.description: When true, the iframe shows scrollbars if content overflows.

maxHeight.description: Maximum height in pixels. null means unconstrained.

maxWidth.description: Maximum width in pixels. null means unconstrained.

minHeight.description: Minimum height in pixels. null means unconstrained.

minWidth.description: Minimum width in pixels. null means unconstrained.

columns.description: Columns in this row. Widths should sum to 12.

additionalClasses.description: CSS class names to apply to the row element.

additionalStyles.description: Inline CSS style strings to apply to the row element.

nodeID.description: ID of the nested component whose own layout to embed.

value.description: Raw HTML string. Sanitized automatically since AP 5.8.

widthXS.description: Column width on extra-small screens (required). 1–12 units; 12 = full width.

widthSM.description: Column width on small screens (≥576px). 1–12 units; inherits widthXS if omitted.

widthMD.description: Column width on medium screens (≥768px). 1–12 units; inherits smaller breakpoint if omitted.

widthLG.description: Column width on large screens (≥992px). 1–12 units; inherits smaller breakpoint if omitted.

widthXL.description: Column width on extra-large screens (≥1200px). 1–12 units; inherits smaller breakpoint if omitted.

additionalClasses.description: CSS class names to apply to the column element.

additionalStyles.description: Inline CSS style strings to apply to the column element.
